_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Grobase cost model — consumed by the wiki, the site cost simulator, and the cost-model gate. Every RAM/capacity number cites a measured artifact (artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json, artifacts/footprint-*.json, artifacts/bench/*.json, artifacts/nano-vs-pocketbase.json) reproducible with `make bench-footprint`/`make bench-capacity`/`make bench-load`. Every price cites a source_url + the date it was read + a confidence flag. NO number here is invented; anything not in an artifact is marked basis_kind/confidence accordingly. COST != PRICE != MARGIN: 'components.mem_basis_mib' + density + storage + egress = the infra COST a node consumes; 'suggested_price_usd_month' is what we'd charge; margin = price - cost. Human/support/on-call/SRE is NOT infra and is the last cost_dimension (driver=note) — never fold it into the per-tenant RAM math. RE-FETCH hoster prices before publishing: cloud prices drift (Hetzner raised 2026-04-01; Fly killed its free tier; AWS revises egress).

cost_dimensions.label: RAM — idle/baseline floor per edition
cost_dimensions.note: Fixed per-node cost regardless of tenant count. nano 2.008 → max 3634 MiB. Src artifacts/footprint-*.json + nano-vs-pocketbase.json. This is the dominant fixed cost on small tiers.

cost_dimensions.label: RAM — per-tenant marginal at rest (the density moat)
cost_dimensions.note: ~0.0001173 MiB/tenant (~0.12 KiB) @ shared_rls with pools_open=0 — 24,888 tenants in a 2.918 MiB data plane. Src artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json. CONDITIONAL: holds only because SHARE_POOLS decouples pool count from tenant count; it is the AT-REST holding cost, not the under-load cost.

cost_dimensions.label: RAM — per-tenant working-set under concurrent load
cost_dimensions.note: ~0.003 MiB per concurrently-loaded tenant (~30 MiB data plane for 10K zipf-concurrent tenants under SHARE_POOLS, server_errors=0). Src artifacts/bench/multitenant-10000.json + m46. This is a function of CONCURRENT working set, not provisioned tenant count — and it is the realistic packing constraint, NOT the at-rest moat.

cost_dimensions.label: vCPU — compute cores
cost_dimensions.note: Bench box = 20 vCPU (nproc) per artifacts/bench/capacity-essential.json. A single mount sustains ~400 read rps at p95<2 ms before the pool cliff (max_sustained_rps=400). vCPU is a priced hoster input; RAM dominates so vCPU is secondary.

cost_dimensions.label: Persistent block storage — Postgres data volume
cost_dimensions.note: Per-tenant row/table data (shared_rls schema) lives on the postgres data volume. Only the postgres RSS (43.99 MiB) is measured — the ON-DISK GB-per-tenant dataset size is NOT in any artifact; modeled via factors.storage_gb_per_tenant_default (ESTIMATED, reasoned from quota_rows).

cost_dimensions.label: WAL + backup storage
cost_dimensions.note: Write-ahead-log retention + logical per-tenant backups (B6, migration 042_tenant_backups.sql, pg-backup sidecar). pg-backup is periodic (mem_limit 128m, no standing RSS). GB figure NOT in any artifact — fold into block_storage or model separately; ESTIMATED.

cost_dimensions.label: Egress / network bandwidth
cost_dimensions.note: Outbound transfer to clients. NOT captured in any current artifact — must be modeled per provider and is marked ESTIMATED in factors.egress_gb_per_tenant_default. Egress is where the 'cheap' clouds (Fly $0.02, Railway $0.05, AWS $0.09) get expensive at scale; Hetzner bundles 20 TB.

cost_dimensions.label: Object storage — MinIO / S3 (storage plane)
cost_dimensions.note: File uploads. MinIO compute RSS is measured (74.4 MiB) but the COST driver is GB STORED, which is not in artifacts. Separate $/GB-month line from block storage. Present pro/max only.

cost_dimensions.label: Observability overhead — Prometheus+Loki+Grafana+promtail+trino
cost_dimensions.note: loki 333.5 + trino 684.1 + debezium 248.1 + prometheus 33 + promtail 56.1 + grafana 47.7 MiB. Src footprint-max.json. Present only in pro/max/prod editions — amortize across tenants. Largest non-DB block in max.

cost_dimensions.label: Realtime + functions runtime
cost_dimensions.note: Realtime router 2.887 MiB RSS (measured); functions-runtime mem_limit 256m, state=DOWN at idle (on-demand). Runtime cost is invocation/connection-driven, not standing — do not count functions-runtime ceiling as steady-state.

cost_dimensions.label: Managed-Postgres HA premium — replica + failover
cost_dimensions.note: Track-C SLA add-on (supavisor pooler 512m + HA replica). NOT a base-compose cost, NO artifact — ESTIMATED. Honest comparator: AWS RDS db.t3.medium single-AZ = $52.56/mo (~1.7x raw EC2 for the same shape) per the hoster snapshot.

cost_dimensions.label: Control-plane + gateway fixed overhead (amortized)
cost_dimensions.note: Go control plane (tenant-control 6.6 + adapter-registry 8.0 + orchestrator 8.8 + webhook-dispatcher 10.1 + function-scheduler 5.0 ≈ 38 MiB) + kong 102.4 + gotrue 7.9 + postgrest 12.0 MiB. Src footprint-live-24888-today.json. Fixed per node, divided across all tenants on it.

cost_dimensions.label: Reserved headroom / safety margin
cost_dimensions.note: Gap between measured RSS and mem_limit ceilings (data plane 2.918 RSS vs 96m limit; postgres 44 RSS vs 512m limit) PLUS burst capacity. factors.headroom_pct=0.30 reserved; advertised rps = floor(measured_ceiling × fair_share × 0.5) per packages.json _measured.

cost_dimensions.label: NON-INFRA — human / support / on-call / SRE
cost_dimensions.note: NOT a server resource. NO artifact. MUST NOT be folded into infra COST or the per-tenant RAM math — bill as a SEPARATE operational line (support tier / SLA / on-call rotation). Flagged distinct from COST vs PRICE vs MARGIN per binding rules.

components.name: realtime (Rust realtime router)
components.source: artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json (RSS) · make bench-footprint EDITION=pro ; mem_limit=128m. NB footprint-pro.json shows 19.6 MiB under that edition's load.

components.name: session-service (TS/Node)
components.source: artifacts/footprint-essential.json (RSS, essential edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=essential ; mem_limit=128m

components.name: schema-service (TS/Node)
components.source: docker-compose.yml mem_limit=128m (no measured RSS isolated; sibling Node services measure ~57-70 MiB RSS — treat 128 as the ceiling budget, not a measured floor)

components.name: analytics-service (TS/Node)
components.source: artifacts/footprint-max.json (RSS, max edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=max ; mem_limit=128m

components.name: functions-runtime (TS/Node fn sandbox)
components.source: docker-compose.yml mem_limit=256m (measured state=down/0 MiB in footprint-max.json — runtime is on-demand, not standing; ceiling is the budget, do NOT count as steady-state)

components.name: kong (API gateway / edge)
components.source: artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json (RSS) · make bench-footprint ; mem_limit=1g. footprint-core.json measured 154.3 MiB, essential/pro 118.5 MiB — varies with load/edition.

components.name: waf (edge WAF)
components.source: artifacts/footprint-essential.json (RSS) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=essential ; mem_limit=256m (footprint-core.json measured 20.1 MiB cold)

components.name: postgres (primary DB)
components.source: artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json (RSS, holding 24,888 tenants shared_rls) · make bench-footprint ; mem_limit=512m

components.name: mysql
components.source: artifacts/footprint-pro.json (RSS, pro edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=pro ; mem_limit=384m

components.name: mariadb
components.source: artifacts/footprint-max.json (RSS, max edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=max ; mem_limit=384m

components.name: minio (object storage / S3)
components.source: artifacts/footprint-pro.json (RSS, pro edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=pro ; mem_limit=512m (docker-compose.prod.yml). NB cost driver is GB stored, not this compute RSS.

components.name: trino (analytics query engine / JVM)
components.source: artifacts/footprint-max.json (RSS, max edition) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=max ; mem_limit=2g — largest single component

components.name: supavisor (connection pooler / multiplexer)
components.source: docker-compose.pooler.yml + docker-compose.prod.yml mem_limit=512m (C1 scale-out seam, not in base editions; no isolated RSS). Supabase's equivalent pooler measured 202.2 MiB in artifacts/bench/supabase-footprint-breakdown.txt for reference.

components.name: pg-backup (logical backup sidecar)
components.source: docker-compose.yml mem_limit=128m (no standing RSS; backup is periodic — do not count as steady-state)

components.name: studio (admin/operator UI)
components.source: docker-compose.yml mem_limit=384m (not in measured editions; dashboard/firewall in prod)

components.name: vault (secrets)
components.source: docker-compose.yml mem_limit=256m (secrets profile; no standing RSS captured in edition footprints)

density.source: artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24888-today.json (2026-06-15, RSS 2.918 MiB / 24,888 tenants, pools_open=0) — reproduces artifacts/scale/footprint-live-24887.json (2026-06-14, 2.6 MiB / 24,887). Reproduce: docker exec mini-baas-postgres psql -tAc 'select count(*) from public.tenants'; docker stats --no-stream data-plane-router; curl 127.0.0.1:4011/metrics | grep pool. THE MOAT: at-rest per-tenant marginal data-plane RAM = 2.918/24888 ≈ 0.000117 MiB (~0.12 KiB) because pools_open=0 (SHARE_POOLS: pool count is independent of tenant count). per_tenant_underload_mib (0.003) = ~30 MiB data plane / 10K concurrently-loaded tenants under zipf with SHARE_POOLS (artifacts/bench/multitenant-10000.json, server_errors=0) — the realistic packing figure.
caveats: AT-REST vs UNDER-LOAD: per_tenant_marginal_mib (0.000117) is the holding cost with pools_open=0; the realistic packing constraint is per_tenant_underload_mib (~0.003) AND the rps fair-share ceiling — NOT the at-rest moat. The simulator must default to the under-load + rps regime for honest tenant-per-node estimates.

SHARE_POOLS IS AN OVERLAY, NOT THE BASE DEFAULT: with it OFF (base compose), the same 10K-tenant zipf load thrashes the pool LRU → 62 server_errors / 12.4% err_pct (artifacts/bench/multitenant-10000-nosharepools-today.json). The density economics REQUIRE DATA_PLANE_SHARE_POOLS=1 (scale overlay / m46). The base shape does not get the moat for free.

SINGLE BOX: all RSS numbers are docker-stats steady-state on one 20-vCPU / ~31,929 MiB / kernel-6.17 machine (capacity-essential.json env). A real cost model must re-measure on the target instance shape.

hosters.name: Hetzner Cloud
hosters._note: CX22 = 2 vCPU/4 GB/40 GB NVMe/20 TB traffic, EUR 3.99/mo @ EUR/USD 1.16 = $4.63 flat. usd_per_gb_ram/usd_per_vcpu are FLAT-PLAN ALLOCATIONS (no separate RAM/CPU SKU). storage+egress=0 because bundled (40 GB disk + 20 TB egress; overage ~EUR 1/TB). Larger CX nodes (8/16/32 GB) used in tiers are DERIVED-linear from this line and marked estimated in the worked_examples.

hosters.name: Hostinger
hosters._note: KVM 2 = 2 vCPU/8 GB/100 GB NVMe/8 TB, $8.99/mo PROMOTIONAL (needs up to 48-mo commit); renewal runs ~2x ($18-21/mo). usd_per_gb_ram=$1.12 is the promo allocation — multiply ~2 for steady-state (ESTIMATED ~$2.2/GB renewal). Not used as a headline node in tiers; kept for the cross-hoster spread.

hosters.name: Fly.io
hosters.representative_plan: shared-cpu-1x 1GB machine (RAM is a true per-GB add-on)
hosters._note: RAM is a real per-GB line (~$5/GB/30d). Reference machines (Amsterdam): 256 MB $2.02, 512 MB $3.32, 1 GB $5.92, 2 GB $11.11. usd_per_vcpu=$2 is ESTIMATED (derived from 256 MB floor; Fly doesn't price shared vCPU separately). Volume $0.15/GB/mo + egress $0.02/GB NA-EU PUBLISHED (NO free allowance post-2025; $0.04 APAC/SA, $0.12 Africa/India). For Fly, node_monthly is computed PER-GB (ram_gb*5 + vcpu*2), not from flat_monthly_usd.

hosters.name: DigitalOcean
hosters._note: 4 GB/2 vCPU/80 GB SSD/4,000 GiB transfer = $24/mo. usd_per_gb_ram=$6/usd_per_vcpu=$12 are flat-plan allocations. Block volumes $0.10/GiB/mo PUBLISHED; egress $0.01/GiB OVERAGE only after a pooled free allowance (≥1,000 GiB) — effective egress ≈ $0 for small workloads. Mid-priced reference, not a headline tier node.

hosters.name: Railway
hosters._note: Fully unit-priced: RAM $10/GB/mo, vCPU $20/vCPU/mo, egress $0.05/GB, volume $0.15/GB/mo — all explicit. flat_monthly_usd $30 illustrative (1 GB + 1 vCPU) on top of a $5 Hobby / $20-per-seat Pro base. Priciest per-GB of the PaaS group; kept for the spread.

hosters.name: AWS (EC2 + RDS reference)
hosters._note: t3.medium = 2 vCPU/4 GB = $0.0416/hr = $30.368/mo. usd_per_gb_ram=$7.59/usd_per_vcpu=$15.18 are whole-instance allocations. EBS gp3 $0.08/GB/mo; egress $0.09/GB first 10 TB (100 GB/mo free). SEPARATE managed-DB ref: RDS db.t3.medium PostgreSQL single-AZ = $52.56/mo on-demand (~1.7x raw EC2) — the honest 'expensive managed Postgres' comparator. Larger t3 nodes in tiers are DERIVED-linear and marked estimated.

factors._factors_note: headroom_pct 0.30 = reserved gap between RSS and node ceiling (also covers burst). overcommit 0.5 = the fair-share factor in packages.json: advertised rps = floor(measured_ceiling × fair_share × 0.5). rps_single_pool_ceiling 400 = artifacts/bench/capacity-essential.json max_sustained_rps (read p95<2ms before the pool cliff). concurrency_peak_fraction 0.10 = ESTIMATED industry default (≈10% of tenants peak simultaneously) used to turn the simultaneous-saturation cap (ceiling/tier_rps) into a realistic tenants_per_node; it is the single biggest tunable assumption — see open_questions. storage_gb_per_tenant_default + egress_gb_per_tenant_default are ESTIMATED (reasoned up from quota_rows tiering) — NO artifact backs per-tenant GB; both must be revisited per real workload. default_margin_pct 0.60 is the BASE/fallback (basic's margin). DECISIONS LOCKED IN 2026-06-16 (the prior open_questions, now resolved): (1) margins are TIERED per margin_pct_by_tier (nano 0 free · basic 0.60 · essential 0.70 · pro 0.80 · max 0.85 — entry-low/premium-high, land-and-expand); (2) concurrency_peak_fraction = 0.10 ADOPTED as the planning default (sensitivity band 0.05-0.20; revisit with production telemetry — it remains the single biggest lever); (3) the per-tenant storage_gb/egress_gb defaults ADOPTED (reasoned up from quota_rows/quota.requests at ~2-4 KB/row+response; revisit per real workload). All three stay tunable in the simulator. Price = cost/(1-tier_margin); margin stays strictly positive on every priced tier×hoster row below; real retail is value-based above the cost floor.

tiers.name: nano
tiers.tenancy: single-tenant (one app per binary; graduates to basic with zero rewrites)
tiers.edition_ram_idle_source: artifacts/nano-vs-pocketbase.json (nano.rss=2.008 MiB, image_mb=4.9) — single static musl binary, NOT a compose edition; reproduce with make nano-build + footprint. vs PocketBase 13.11 MiB RSS / 30.1 MB binary same box.
components: binocle-nano (single static musl binary, FROM scratch; embedded SQLite + in-process auth + SSE realtime)

tenants_per_node.basis: rps-bound (not RAM): ceiling/tier_rps = 400/50 = 8 simultaneous-peak × concurrency_peak_fraction 0.10⁻¹ = 80. RAM at-rest would allow ~6.1M on a 1 GB Fly node (the moat), so rps binds first by ~5 orders of magnitude. Single-tenant tier: tenants_per_node only applies in the SHARED/amortized model; dedicated = 1.

tiers.infra_cost_basis: DEDICATED node (whole box for one app), storage+egress at tier defaults. Hetzner = whole-box allocation attributed (flat $4.63 box but only ~$1.16 of RAM-share needed for 1 GB-class; we model nano on a 1 GB-equivalent allocation = $1.16). Fly = ram 1GB×5 + vcpu 1×2 = $7 + $0.075 stor + $0.01 egr ≈ $7.0. AWS = 1 GB-class t3 share $7.59 + $0.008 + $0.045 ≈ $7.64. See worked_examples for the amortized (÷80) numbers.
tiers.suggested_price_note: FREE TIER by product design (packages.json nano _comment: 'The free-tier shape'). Price 0 / margin 0 is intentional, NOT a model error — nano is the loss-leader acquisition tier. Amortized infra cost is $0.04-0.37/tenant/mo (see worked_examples), affordable as CAC. If ever monetized, dedicated price would be ~$2.90 (Hetzner) per the cost/(1-0.6) rule.

tiers.name: basic
tiers.tenancy: single-tenant (a private single-app BaaS on a Pi or $5 VPS)
tiers.edition_ram_idle_source: artifacts/footprint-basic.json (ram_mib_total=309.8, bar_mib=512, verdict=pass) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=basic

tenants_per_node.basis: rps-bound: 400/100 = 4 simultaneous × 0.10⁻¹ = 40. RAM at-rest allows ~3.5M on a 1 GB node; rps binds first. Edition floor 309.8 MiB fits a 1 GB node with 30% headroom (716.8 MiB avail). Single-tenant tier: dedicated=1; 40 is the shared/amortized cap.

tiers.infra_cost_basis: DEDICATED 1 GB-class node + tier storage/egress. Edition fits a 1 GB node. Hetzner allocation $1.16, Fly 1GB×5+1vcpu×2=$7.0(+stor/egr), AWS 1GB-class $7.59. Amortized ÷40 in worked_examples.
tiers.suggested_price_note: Dedicated on Hetzner: cost $1.16 → price $1.16/(1-0.6) = $2.90, margin 60%. On Fly dedicated: $7.0 → $17.50. Amortized (÷40) the floor is ~$0.07-0.80/tenant → trivially profitable at $2.90. Matches the packages.json 'private single-app on a $5 VPS' framing (our cost is below $5).

tiers.name: essential
tiers.edition_ram_idle_source: artifacts/footprint-essential.json (ram_mib_total=821.7, bar_mib=1024, verdict=pass) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=essential

tenants_per_node.basis: rps-bound: 400/200 = 2 simultaneous × 0.10⁻¹ = 20. Edition floor 821.7 MiB does NOT fit a 1 GB node (Fly 1 GB has only 716.8 MiB after headroom — node-too-small) → MUST use ≥2 GB. On 2 GB: 1434 MiB avail, RAM allows ~5.2M at rest; rps binds. Single-tenant: dedicated=1; 20 amortized.

tiers.infra_cost_basis: DEDICATED 2 GB-class node (edition needs >1 GB). Hetzner 2 GB allocation $2.31 (derived from CX22 $1.16/GB-equiv), Fly 2GB×5+1vcpu×2=$12.0(+stor/egr), AWS 2GB-class t3 $15.19. Amortized ÷20 in worked_examples.
tiers.suggested_price_note: Tiered margin 70% (premium ladder: entry tiers lower, premium higher). Dedicated Hetzner cost floor $2.31 → $7.70. Amortized ÷20 is far cheaper ($0.29-2.55/tenant); Fly/AWS dedicated run higher — the simulator shows the live 3-hoster spread. This is the cost-justified floor; real retail is value-based above it. The capability differentiator over basic is aggregate/count-sum-group-by.

tiers.name: pro
tiers.tenancy: one customer's SaaS, amortizable across a shared host (< $1/tenant per packages.json)
tiers.edition_ram_idle_source: artifacts/footprint-pro.json (ram_mib_total=1188.4, bar_mib=1500, verdict=pass) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=pro

tenants_per_node.basis: rps-bound: 400/400 = 1 simultaneous × 0.10⁻¹ = 10. Edition floor 1188.4 MiB needs >1 GB (fits 2 GB: 1434.8 MiB avail). RAM at rest would allow ~2.1M; rps binds hardest here (a single pro tenant at peak can saturate the node's pool). The packages.json '<$1/tenant amortized' claim matches: $2.31 node ÷ 10 = $0.23 Hetzner.

tiers.infra_cost_basis: 2 GB-class node (edition idle 1188 MiB + headroom). NB engine-mask-vs-containers: pro REGISTERS mariadb/cockroachdb mounts but those CONTAINERS run only at max (engines-extra profile) — base pro stack does NOT start them, so this RAM does not include mariadb/cockroach/mssql. Amortized ÷10 in worked_examples.
tiers.suggested_price_note: Tiered margin 80%. Dedicated Hetzner cost floor $2.31 → $11.55. Amortized ÷10 = $0.23/tenant on Hetzner (confirms the packages.json '<$1/tenant') but $2.15 on Fly / $7.05 on AWS — the hoster choice flips the unit economics (the simulator surfaces this). Cost-justified floor; real retail is value-based above it.

tiers.name: max
tiers.tenancy: MULTI-TENANT PLATFORM (the 10K-tenant validation backs the density story; ONLY tier sold as multi-tenant)
tiers.edition_ram_idle_source: artifacts/footprint-max.json (ram_mib_total=3634.0, bar_mib=3700, verdict=pass) · make bench-footprint PACKAGE=max

tenants_per_node.basis: THE MULTI-TENANT TIER — this is where the moat pays off. Edition floor 3634 MiB needs ≥8 GB (5734.4 MiB avail after headroom on 8 GB). At-rest RAM allows ~1.9M tenants (5734/0.000117); UNDER LOAD the proven figure is 10K concurrent tenants in ~30 MiB data plane (multitenant-10000.json, server_errors=0, REQUIRES SHARE_POOLS). We cap tenants_per_node at the PROVEN 10,000 (m46 headline), NOT the theoretical millions. rps is NOT the binding constraint here because max assumes the per-tier DATA_PLANE_MAX_POOLS policy + supavisor multiplexing (B4); RAM working-set + the proven 10K validation bind.

tiers.infra_cost_basis: 8 GB-class node holding the full max edition. Hetzner 8 GB ~$7.0 (derived from cost-optimized line; ESTIMATED above CX22), Fly 8GB×5+4vcpu×2=$48.0, AWS t3.large/8GB-class $60.74 (derived-linear). These are the NODE costs; per-tenant = node ÷ 10,000 + storage + egress. mssql(422.5)+cockroach(413.9)+trino(684.1)+loki(333.5)+debezium(248.1) dominate the floor.
tiers.suggested_price_note: Tiered margin 85% (top tier). DEDICATED private-platform cost floor: Hetzner $7.0 → $46.67. But max is the MULTI-TENANT tier: per-tenant cost = node ÷ proven-10K + storage/egress = $0.0007 (Hetzner) to $6.11 (AWS, where egress+storage dominate, not compute) → see worked_examples. Per-tenant retail is the published tier rate, not this floor; margin is enormous at density.

formulas.node_ram_needed_mib: node_ram_needed(N, tier) = component_ram_sum_mib(tier) + N * density.per_tenant_marginal_mib   // AT REST. Under concurrent load substitute density.per_tenant_underload_mib for the active fraction: component_ram_sum + (N * concurrency_peak_fraction) * per_tenant_underload_mib.
formulas.tenants_per_node_ram: tenants_per_node_ram(tier, hoster) = floor( (hoster.ram_gb*1024*(1-headroom_pct) - edition_ram_idle_mib) / per_tenant_marginal_mib )   // the AT-REST moat ceiling — astronomically large (millions); NOT the realistic cap.
formulas.tenants_per_node_rps: tenants_per_node_rps(tier) = floor( (rps_single_pool_ceiling / tier.rps) / concurrency_peak_fraction )   // realistic cap from the measured 400-rps single-pool ceiling and a 10%-peak assumption. = ceiling/rps simultaneous-saturating tenants, scaled up by 1/peak.
formulas.tenants_per_node: tenants_per_node(tier, hoster) = min( tenants_per_node_rps(tier), tenants_per_node_ram(tier, hoster) ), then for `max` CAP at the PROVEN 10,000 (multitenant-10000.json / m46) rather than the theoretical RAM millions. For single-tenant tiers (nano/basic/essential) the DEDICATED model is 1; the amortized model uses this value.
formulas.node_monthly_for_node: node_monthly(hoster, ram_gb, vcpu) = flat_monthly_usd if the representative flat plan fits the needed ram_gb, ELSE (ram_gb*usd_per_gb_ram_month + vcpu*usd_per_vcpu_month). Fly is always computed per-GB (ram_gb*5 + vcpu*2) since RAM is a true add-on; Hetzner/AWS larger-than-representative nodes are DERIVED-linear from the representative $/GB and flagged estimated.
formulas.infra_cost_per_tenant_dedicated: infra_cost_dedicated(tier, hoster) = node_monthly(hoster, node_ram_gb, vcpu) + storage_gb_per_tenant_default[tier]*usd_per_gb_storage_month + egress_gb_per_tenant_default[tier]*usd_per_gb_egress   // whole node attributed to one app.
formulas.infra_cost_per_tenant_amortized: infra_cost_amortized(tier, hoster) = node_monthly(...) / tenants_per_node(tier, hoster) + storage_gb_per_tenant_default[tier]*usd_per_gb_storage_month + egress_gb_per_tenant_default[tier]*usd_per_gb_egress   // node split across packed tenants.
formulas.suggested_price: suggested_price(cost) = cost / (1 - default_margin_pct)   // default_margin_pct=0.60 → price = cost*2.5. margin_pct = (price - cost)/price = default_margin_pct. Constraint: price > cost ALWAYS (margin strictly positive); nano is the deliberate exception (free tier, margin 0).
formulas.human_support_line: NON-INFRA: support/on-call/SRE is added as a SEPARATE operational line per tier (support SLA), never inside infra_cost_* and never inside the per-tenant RAM math. No artifact backs it; it is a business input.

worked_examples.arithmetic: node_ram_needed(1, basic) = 309.8 + 1*0.0001173 ≈ 309.8 MiB → fits a 1 GB node (716.8 MiB avail after 30% headroom). node_monthly = $1.16 (1 GB-class Hetzner allocation). storage 0.5 GB × $0 + egress 1.0 GB × $0 = $0 (both bundled). infra_cost_dedicated = $1.16. suggested_price = 1.16/(1-0.60) = $2.90. margin = (2.90-1.16)/2.90 = 60%.

tenants_per_node_rps(basic) = (400/100)/0.10 = 40. node_monthly(Fly, 1GB,1vcpu) = 1*5 + 1*2 = $7.00. per-tenant node share = 7.00/40 = $0.175. storage 0.5×$0.15 = $0.075 + egress 1.0×$0.02 = $0.02 → infra_cost_amortized = $0.270. suggested_price = 0.270/(1-0.60) = $0.68. margin 60%.

edition floor 821.7 MiB does NOT fit 1 GB (Fly 1 GB only 716.8 avail) → use 2 GB node. node_monthly = $2.31 (2 GB Hetzner derived-linear from $1.16/GB-equiv). storage/egress bundled = $0. infra_cost_dedicated = $2.31. suggested_price = 2.31/0.40 = $5.77. margin 60%.

tenants_per_node_rps(essential) = (400/200)/0.10 = 20. node_monthly(AWS 2GB-class t3) = $15.19 (derived-linear from t3.medium $30.37/4GB). per-tenant node share = 15.19/20 = $0.760. storage 1.0×$0.08 = $0.08 + egress 2.0×$0.09 = $0.18 → infra_cost_amortized = $1.019. suggested_price = 1.019/0.40 = $2.55. margin 60%.

tenants_per_node_rps(pro) = (400/400)/0.10 = 10. edition floor 1188.4 MiB → 2 GB node, node_monthly = $2.31. per-tenant node share = 2.31/10 = $0.231. storage 5.0×$0 + egress 10.0×$0 = $0 (Hetzner bundled). infra_cost_amortized = $0.231 → CONFIRMS packages.json '<$1/tenant'. suggested_price = 0.231/0.40 = $0.58. margin 60%.

node_monthly(Fly 2GB,1vcpu) = 2*5+1*2 = $12.00. per-tenant node share = 12.00/10 = $1.20. storage 5.0×$0.15 = $0.75 + egress 10.0×$0.02 = $0.20 → infra_cost_amortized = $2.15. suggested_price = 2.15/0.40 = $5.37. margin 60%. NB on Fly the pro '<$1/tenant' claim is FALSE ($2.15) — hoster choice flips it; the simulator must surface this.

edition floor 3634 MiB → 8 GB node (5734.4 MiB avail after headroom). PROVEN cap = 10,000 tenants (multitenant-10000.json, server_errors=0, SHARE_POOLS ON). node_monthly(Hetzner 8GB) = $7.00 (derived, estimated). per-tenant node share = 7.00/10000 = $0.0007. storage 20×$0 + egress 50×$0 = $0 (Hetzner bundled 20 TB). infra_cost_amortized ≈ $0.0007/tenant. At this density per-tenant compute is ~free; storage/egress dominate on metered clouds. Retail price set by published tier rate; margin enormous.

node_monthly(AWS 8GB t3-class) = $60.74 (derived-linear). per-tenant node share = 60.74/10000 = $0.006. storage 20×$0.08 = $1.60 + egress 50×$0.09 = $4.50 → infra_cost_amortized = $6.106/tenant — DOMINATED by storage+egress, not compute (compute is $0.006). This is the honest lesson: at the moat density, AWS's $0.09/GB egress + $0.08/GB storage, not RAM, set the floor. suggested_price = 6.106/0.40 = $15.27. margin 60%. (Contrast Hetzner ~$0.0007 because egress is bundled.)

A customer renting a PRIVATE max platform: node_monthly(Fly 8GB,4vcpu) = 8*5+4*2 = $48.00. storage 20×$0.15 = $3.00 + egress 50×$0.02 = $1.00 → infra_cost_dedicated = $52.00. suggested_price = 52.00/0.40 = $130.00. margin 60%.
